old_claim.note: Horticulture provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Advanced Hunting & Gathering fits the Natufian settlement anchor: the Met describes year-round villages of hunters and foragers relying on local gazelle, wild cereals, and marine resources rather than cultivated horticulture.

source_shape.source_locator: The Met describes Natufians as hunters and foragers who settled in villages year-round and relied on local resources including gazelle, wild cereals, and marine life.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors permanent Natufian villages to hunter-forager subsistence, not horticulture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected edge uses the source-backed forager lifeway package already represented by advanced_hunting_gathering.

ontology_before: Village Settlements depended on Horticulture, implying cultivation enabled the Eynan/Ain Mallaha anchor despite the source describing settled hunters and foragers.
ontology_after: Village Settlements depends on Advanced Hunting & Gathering and Basic Shelter, matching the Natufian hunter-forager settlement scope.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: village_settlements now depends on advanced_hunting_gathering and no longer directly depends on horticulture.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to Neolithic farming villages rather than Natufian permanent or semi-permanent settlements.
A source directly shows horticulture or cultivation at Eynan/Ain Mallaha as the enabling basis for the stored date.
advanced_hunting_gathering is removed without adding another source-backed hunter-forager settlement context.
short_reason: The settlement anchor is source-backed as hunter-forager, not horticultural.
